title: Fifteen Years of the Premier League: What Sustained Success Looks Like
summary: A long-run Premier League study moving from clean historical data to fair comparisons, performance efficiency and predictive modelling.
key_takeaway: Sustained football success is not explained by league tables alone: fair per-game comparisons, balanced performance and controlled aggression reveal a richer picture.

inline_figures.caption: The strongest Premier League teams combine attacking output with defensive control, converting that balance into points per game.
Winner discipline varies by season, so yellow cards are a feature to interpret in context rather than a simple explanation for success.
